title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Bearer Token Scope
summary: A multi-tenant operator client rotates short-lived bearer tokens and isolated cookie jars without logging or forwarding credentials. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go bearer token scope increment in a Go 1.26 net/http least-authority credential and session policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Acquire, attach, scope, refresh, expire, and revoke bearer tokens without treating possession as identity proof. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Bearer Token Scope · Theory Bearer Token Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model handles host-only session cookie SID-6, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Define jar isolation, CSRF, scope, rotation, redaction, and redirect boundaries. Competency probe: Acquire, attach, scope, refresh, expire, and revoke bearer tokens without treating possession as identity proof. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
